summary: Bound the rendered human action surface to the rule engine so bidding, contract ordering, widow pickup, discard confirmation, whist decisions, play-card clicks, settlement, and next-deal continuation expose only legal actions. Removed the active-game new-deal bypass from the gameplay UI and suppressed clickable rule controls when the actor is AI.

tests_run.result: failed before fix, then passed after fix
tests_run.evidence: Red run failed 8 assertions because rendered controls lacked legal-action identities, still showed the active-game bypass path, and discard confirmation was not rule-engine bound. Green run passed 12 render tests covering bidding, contract order, widow pickup, discard, whist, play, settlement, next-deal, finished, and AI actor states.
Focused UI/controller verification passed 21 tests, including the preserved US-841 start/resume flow and removal of the in-game new-deal bypass button.
Vitest passed 10 files / 94 tests and the production build completed successfully with tsc and vite build.

node_package_scripts.verify: npm run test && npm run build

evidence_matrix.scenario: Fixed-profile whist and all-pass continuation controls
evidence_matrix.expected: UI shows pass/whist/half-whist only where the profile allows them, shows check-only for ten-level games, and exposes only settle/next-deal actions for post-play continuation.
evidence_matrix.evidence: src/ui/render.test.ts covers standard whist, ten-level check, and all-pass settlement/next-deal rendering via getLegalActions-backed action identities; npm run test -- src/ui/render.test.ts src/ui/controller.test.ts passed.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Human action surface stays inside legal rule actions for every supported phase
evidence_matrix.expected: Bidding, contract order, widow pickup, discard confirmation, misere play, contract play, settlement, and next-deal controls are a subset or projection of getLegalActions(state).
evidence_matrix.evidence: src/ui/render.test.ts asserts rendered action keys against getLegalActions for bidding, contract order, widow pickup, contract play, misere play, settlement, next-deal, finished, and AI actor states; discard confirmation only emits a legal discardCards action after a legal pair is selected.

evidence_matrix.scenario: UI legal action surface and invalid-control suppression
evidence_matrix.expected: Invalid actions are not displayed as clickable controls, active gameplay does not expose a bypassing new-deal action, and AI-actor turns do not expose clickable human rule actions.
evidence_matrix.evidence: src/ui/render.ts now omits the active-game data-new-game control, tags legal action buttons with stable identities, disables non-legal play cards, and suppresses clickable rule controls for AI actors; src/ui/controller.test.ts and src/ui/render.test.ts verify the behavior.
